content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive, Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

June 9, 1978

Friends of Mike Curb
Post Office Box 15077-C
Sacramento, California 95831

Dear Friends For Mike Curb:

     Enclosed is a copy of a letter and an envelope sent
to the Fair Political Practices Commission which appears
to be in violation of Government Code Section 84305.

     Section 84305 says that the sender of a mass mailing
must show on the outside of each piece of mail in the mass
mailing and on at least one of the inserts within each
piece of mail the sender's name.

     On the envelope and on an insert you listed F.M.C.,
which apparently are initials for "Friends for Mike Curb."

     Although we do not plan to take any action on this
mailer, we wanted to inform you that you must list the
entire name of the sender on the envelope and one insert
within the mailer.

     We are confident that in the future you will comply
with the provisions of Government Code Section 84305.
Please call on me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern
General Counsel

RMS:nc

Enclosure

DISTRICT OFFICE
735 W. DUARTE ROAD, SUITE 207
ARCADIA, CALIFORNIA 91006
(213) 445-4404

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
95814
(916) 445-3668

COMMITTEES
ELECTIONS
REAPPORTIONMENT, VICE CHAIR
AGRICULTURE AND
WATER RESOURCES
EDUCATION
REVENUE AND TAXATION
SENATE SELECT COMMITTEE
ON PENAL INSTITUTIONS

STATE SENATOR

H. L. RICHARDSON

TWENTY-FIFTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT

VICE CHAIRMAN
COMMITTEE ON ELECTIONS AND REAPPORTIONMENT

California State Senate

MR. Thomas Quinn
2743 Marty Way
Sacramento, CA 95818

Dear Mr. Quinn:

I am writing to you personally because it is vital that
I know whether or not you believe Jerry Brown and Mervyn
Dymally can be defeated in 1978.

That's why I have enclosed an individually numbered
confidential VOTER PREFERENCE TALLY SHEET which I urge you
to complete and return as soon as possible. Your registration number is 905-174933.

The results will be immediately tabulated to develop
what professional campaign managers refer to as a "profile"
on the Ultra-Liberal Administration of Jerry Brown and
Mervyn Dymally.

A profile shows how voters feel about key issues.
And it rates how the Administration is doing in such critical areas as law enforcement, energy, jobs development
and property tax relief.

A good profile on the opposition (in this case, Governor Brown and Lieutenant Governor Dymally) is absolutely
essential for conservative Republican candidates who hope
to defeat these Ultra-Liberals in November.

That's why I strongly urge you to use your VOTER PREFERENCE TALLY SHEET to make sure that we can prepare as
complete a profile as possible on the Brown-Dymally Administration.

It is particularly important that we have this information on California's Lieutenant Governor, Mervyn Dymally.

Let me tell you why.

The national news media and the professional pollsters
are already giving heavy coverage to the California Governor's race. But they have virtually ignored Mervyn Dymally,
who has one of the most important jobs in the state!

As Lieutenant Governor, Mervyn Dymally is responsible
for the development of new jobs and new business in California

(over)

Page 3

And he still finds time to fulfill his duties as Calif-
ornia's Republican National Committeeman, serving as the
youngest member ever to sit on the RNC's Executive Board.

I believe that Mike Curb is exactly the kind of aggres-
sive, young Conservative leader that we need to breathe new
life into conservative and Republican principles that
you and I cherish.

While the Brown-Dymally Administration has bent over
backwards to drive business and jobs to other states, Mike
knows that new business and new jobs go hand in hand.

The environmental extremists hold the reins of power
in the Brown-Dymally Administration. But Mike believes that
we must intelligently balance the needs of the environment
and the economy.

As Lt. Governor, Mike Curb would act aggressively to
assure that California's critical energy needs are met.
This Administration's "fantasyland" approach to our energy
needs cannot be allowed to endanger the jobs and economic
security of the people of California.

In the area of crime control, Mike Curb believes that
the time has come to clamp down hard on the violent, repeat
offenders.

On re-instating the Death Penalty, the issue is clear-
cut again--Mike Curb favors the Death Penalty for vicious
murderers--the Brown-Dymally Administration does not.

In short, I know that you and I could count on Mike
Curb to stand firm whenever the Lt. Governor is called upon
to cast the tie-breaker vote in the State Senate.

But if Mike Curb is going to successfully unseat
Mervyn Dymally, he's going to need your personal involve-
ment. And that's why I sincerely hope that you will do
these three things for Mike:

1)    Complete your VOTER PREFERENCE TALLY SHEET at once
     and return it directly to the FRIENDS FOR MIKE CURB
     campaign headquarters, using the enclosed postage-
     free envelope.

2)    Indicate on the bottom of your Tally Sheet whether
     or not you will be able to join Mike's network of
     volunteers, along with such people as Efrem Zimbalist,
     Jr., Bob Hope, Wayne Newton, and Pat Boone.

3)    Make a contribution to Mike's campaign so that he will
     have a fighting chance against the huge campaign war-
     chest that the Liberal Democrats are putting together
     to help Mervyn Dymally.

(over)

sections.conclusion: Although we do not plan to take any action on this mailer, we wanted to inform you that you must list the entire name of the sender on the envelope and one insert within the mailer.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a political committee's mass mailing comply with Government Code Section 84305 requirements regarding sender identification on mail pieces?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC found that the committee's use of initials 'F.M.C.' on the envelope and insert violated Section 84305, which requires the full sender name to be displayed. The committee must comply with this requirement in future mailings.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two separate letters: an FPPC advice letter from Robert M. Stern at top, followed by an unrelated campaign letter from H.L. Richardson. Analysis focuses on the FPPC advice letter portion. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION headers present, but content clearly indicates advisory nature of response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does a political committee's mass mailing comply with Government Code Section 84305 requirements regarding sender identification on mail pieces?

CONCLUSION: Although we do not plan to take any action on this mailer, we wanted to inform you that you must list the entire name of the sender on the envelope and one insert within the mailer.

embedding.summary: The FPPC General Counsel notified the Friends for Mike Curb committee that their mass mailing violated Government Code Section 84305 by using only initials instead of the full committee name on the envelope and inserts, and instructed them to comply with the full name requirement in future mailings.
